channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Green Card Seekers Must Leave U.S. to Apply, Trump Administration Says
item.description: The change is likely to affect hundreds of thousands of people. It could also lead to more family separations as spouses or relatives wait for application decisions, immigration lawyers said.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo and Albert Sun
item.media:credit: Libby March for The New York Times
item.media:description: There are various pathways for foreigners to obtain green cards, which grant them the ability to live and work in the United States as permanent residents.

item.title: Judge Dismisses Criminal Case Against Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia
item.description: The move deals an embarrassing blow to the Trump administration, which made the Maryland man, Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia, the face of its deportation campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia outside a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement in Baltimore last year.

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil to Appeal to Supreme Court in Effort to Halt Deportation
item.description: The move was expected to come after a federal appeals court on Friday ruled against Mr. Khalil, who became the face of President Trump’s crackdown on pro-Palestinian protesters.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich
item.media:credit: Scott Heins for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mahmoud Khalil will likely ask the Supreme Court to review the decision to deport him after a lower court declined to hear his case.

item.title: Trump Weighs His Options in Carrying Out New Strikes in Iran
item.description: There is no shortage of targets if he decides to strike: Energy facilities left untouched, the deep underground nuclear storage site at Isfahan and missile sites that appear to have been dug out.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Eric Schmitt, Tyler Pager, Jonathan Swan and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump’s announcement on Friday that he was skipping the wedding this weekend of his son Donald Trump Jr. came as negotiations with Iran over its nuclear program and its blockage of the Strait of Hormuz appeared to have hit a dead end.

item.title: Once Trump’s Co-Pilot Against Iran, Netanyahu Is Now a Mere Passenger
item.description: A partner in the war, Israel has been largely left out of the peace talks, a humbling setback for its prime minister with significant risks for the country.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel with President Trump at Mar-a-Lago in December.

item.title: The Risks of Iran’s Threat to Control the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran is trying to assert its control over the strait by charging for passage. Experts say it is unlikely to happen, but the threat has unsettled the shipping industry.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Majid Saeedi/Getty Images
item.media:description: Ships anchored in the Strait of Hormuz earlier this month. Vessels and workers have been stranded in the Persian Gulf for nearly three months.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard Resigns as Director of National Intelligence. Trump Names Aaron Lukas Acting Chief.
item.description: Ms. Gabbard had a difficult tenure in the Trump administration and was seldom seen in the room when the president made important national-security moves.
item.dc:creator: Dustin Volz and Julian E. Barnes
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Tulsi Gabbard at a House hearing in March. She said on Friday that she would remain in her post as the nation’s intelligence chief until June 30.

item.title: White House Approves $9 Billion for Spy Agencies to Catch Up on A.I.
item.description: The C.I.A. and N.S.A. cannot fully deploy the latest models on their classified systems because of a shortage of cutting-edge chips.
item.dc:creator: Dustin Volz and Julian E. Barnes
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jensen Huang, president and CEO of NVIDIA, gives a presentation in Washington last October.

item.title: How a Chemical Tank in Southern California Came to the Brink of Disaster
item.description: Firefighters are left without any options to prevent the tank, filled with a toxic chemical, from collapsing or exploding. The authorities have urged thousands of people to evacuate their homes.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla
item.media:credit: Caroline Brehman/Reuters
item.media:description: A blocked-off road in Garden Grove, Calif., on Friday.

item.title: What Is Methyl Methacrylate, the Chemical at the Center of the Garden Grove Crisis?
item.description: The E.P.A. warns that short-term exposure to the compound can cause skin and eye irritation and respiratory issues.
item.dc:creator: Emily Baumgaertner Nunn
item.media:credit: Alessia Pierdomenico/Bloom, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Methyl methacrylate is widely used to manufacture resins and acrylic plastics. It can be found in products like advertising signs, light fixtures, plumbing and latex paint.

item.title: ‘Colbert’ Is Done. ‘SNL’ is Stale. Where Does Late-Night TV Go From Here? | Cannonball
item.description: Maybe “Saturday Night Live” should go instead.
item.dc:creator: Wesley Morris and Jason Zinoman
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by The New York Times; Photo by Caro Scarimbolo/NBC via Getty Images
item.media:description: Mikey Day, Marcello Hernández and Colin Jost on the “Weekend Update” segment of “Saturday Night Live.”

item.title: Colbert’s Final ‘Late Show’ Draws 6.7 Million Viewers
item.description: The total tripled his usual audience this season. But it is only about half of what Jay Leno and David Letterman attracted for their final episodes.
item.dc:creator: John Koblin
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: G.O.P. Break With Trump Reflects Limits of Party’s Post-Jan. 6 Truce
item.description: Republicans, seeing President Trump’s personal agenda diverging from their political interests, vented their outrage about paying those who threatened their lives on Jan. 6, 2021.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Bill Cassidy, Republican of Louisiana, at the Capitol this week after losing his primary to a Trump-backed challenger.

item.title: HUD Moves to Limit Assistance Animals for Disabled Tenants
item.description: Housing officials were told to exclude emotional support animals and tighten the definition of a service animal when granting accommodations for disabled tenants.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron and Debra Kamin
item.media:credit: Jason Kempin/Getty Images
item.media:description: A service dog at the 2023 Veterans Day parade in Nashville.

item.title: Lawsuit Argues Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund Excludes Those He Targeted
item.description: The constellation of individuals and groups involved claims to have suffered partisan attacks by the federal government under Trump, yet would not be compensated.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague
item.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rioters during the attack at the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021.

item.title: Their Phones Were Stolen in London. Then the Threats Started.
item.description: Tens of thousands of smartphones were reported stolen in the British capital in recent years. For some victims, losing their phone was only the beginning.
item.dc:creator: Lizzie Dearden and Amelia Nierenberg
item.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Alex Pikula at home in Chicago. He had his phone stolen while on a trip to London in May 2024, after which his mother started receiving strange and increasingly threatening texts.

item.title: Xi Calls for All-Out Rescue After Coal Mine Explosion Kills at Least 90 in China
item.description: The death count rose drastically on Saturday as the Chinese leader, Xi Jinping, called for an investigation and emphasized the need to “hold those responsible to account.”
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.media:credit: Cnsphoto, via Reuters
item.media:description: Rescuers at the site of a deadly explosion at the Liushenyu coal mine in China’s Shanxi Province, on Saturday.

item.title: The D.N.C. Chairman, Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund and More Politics News
item.description: He has focused on internal politics, building a loyal base despite tactical disputes.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Jenn Ackerman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ken Martin has fulfilled a campaign promise to dispense more funds to each of the D.N.C.’s state parties.

item.title: Trump Visits New York to Boost House Republican’s Re-election Hopes
item.description: The visit by President Trump was meant to promote Representative Mike Lawler’s bid to defend his swing seat in New York, but it had the earmarks of a Trump rally.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford
item.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump appeared at his first rally in New York since his 2024 election to campaign for Representative Mike Lawler.

item.title: Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson Wed in West Palm Beach
item.description: President Trump’s eldest son was married in Palm Beach, Fla. The president described celebrations this weekend as a “small, little private affair.”
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan and Tammy LaGorce
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson were legally married on Thursday in West Palm Beach, Fla., according to Palm Beach County marriage records.

item.title: In Idaho’s Suburbs, a Rat Invasion Tests the Limits of Small Government
item.description: Irrigation canals around Boise have served as a rat superhighway, bringing an infestation so serious that health officials have floated declaring a public emergency.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin
item.media:credit: Loren Elliott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Aldo Guerrero, a pest technician, looks toward a rat bait station set next to a home by a canal in Boise, Idaho.

item.title: Deadly Explosions Injure Dozens of Firefighters on Staten Island Barge
item.description: Two explosions rocked a barge on the North Shore of Staten Island, injuring 34 people. The resulting fire was under control, but was still burning.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag and Nicol León Arge

item.title: Mob Burns Congo Ebola Center Amid Rare Strain Outbreak
item.description: Several hundred people massed at the gates of a hospital to demand the body of a suspected Ebola victim. Violence broke out when staff refused the request.
item.dc:creator: Declan Walsh and Arlette Bashizi
item.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times

item.title: U.S. to Block Entry to More Noncitizens Who May Have Been Exposed to Ebola
item.description: The Trump administration announced plans to expand a ban on entering the United States to legal permanent residents who had been in the Democratic Republic of Congo, Uganda or South Sudan.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli and Hamed Aleaziz
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Health care workers check temperatures as people pass through a checkpoint in Goma, Democratic Republic of Congo, on Thursday.

item.title: Dan Pfeiffer Thinks Two Democrats Could Upend American Politics
item.description: Why 2026 might tell us a lot about 2028.
item.dc:creator: Dan Pfeiffer and John Guida
item.media:credit: Daniel Ribar for The New York Times

item.title: The Simple Answer to Taxing the Rich Is the Best Answer
item.description: Forget complex schemes. There is an easier way to get revenue from the wealthy.
item.dc:creator: Zachary Liscow

item.title: Disconnect Widens Between Mamdani and Pro-Israel Jewish Leaders
item.description: In recent days, Mayor Zohran Mamdani has taken actions that have made the relationship between his office and some Jewish New York leaders more fraught.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack and Sally Goldenberg
item.media:credit: Adam Gray for The New York Times
item.media:description: An annual parade on Fifth Avenue in Manhattan celebrating Israel typically attracts the mayor of New York City, but Zohran Mamdani has said he plans to skip the event this year.

item.title: Rob Base, Rapper Known for ‘It Takes Two,’ Dies at 59
item.description: The singer died on Friday after a “private battle with cancer,” according to his official Instagram account.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk
item.media:credit: Rob Grabowski/Invision, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The rapper Rob Base in 2022. He was known for the hit song “It Takes Two.”

item.title: SpaceX Completes Mostly Successful Starship Rocket Flight
item.description: The 12th test flight of SpaceX’s gargantuan rocket launched on Friday evening and ended its journey in the Indian Ocean just over an hour later.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang
item.media:credit: Eric Gay/Associated Press
item.media:description: Sunrise at Starbase in South Texas on Friday, as the SpaceX Starship rocket was preparing for another launch attempt.

item.title: When Humans Went Away, the Wildlife Strayed
item.description: When people disappeared from the landscape, as they did during the pandemic, wild animals changed how they used space and resources, scientists found.
item.dc:creator: Emily Anthes
item.media:credit: Loren Elliott for The New York Times
item.media:description: A mule deer captured by a trail camera as it walked along a game trail in the Verdugo Mountains in Glendale, Calif.
